data.title: USA TODAY GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.22, revenue of $545.5M misses by $5.51M
data.description:
• USA TODAY press release (TDAY): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.12 beats by $0.22.
  • Revenue of $545.5M (-4.6% Y/Y) misses by $5.51M.
  • Total digital revenues of $261.9 million, or 47.8% of total revenues, up 5.2% year-over-year on a same store basis(1)
  • Net income attributable to USA TODAY Co. of $19.9 million, an increase of $27.2 million year-over-year, reflecting a margin of 3.6%
  • Total Adjusted EBITDA(1) of $73.1 million, an increase of 44.7% year-over-year

data.title: Arrow Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.06, revenue of $44.76M beats by $0.96M
data.description:
• Arrow Financial press release (AROW): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $44.76M (+14.2% Y/Y) beats by $0.96M.
  • Efficiency ratio of 59.89%; 58.13% excluding MRE1
  • Net Interest Income of $36.1 million
  • Net Interest Margin improved to 3.47% (3.48% FTE2), from 3.24% (3.25% FTE) in the prior quarter
  • Return on Average Assets (ROA) of 1.23%; 1.29% adjusted for MRE

data.title: Slide expects $1.85B-$1.95B in 2026 gross written premiums while preparing a ~$3.5B first event reinsurance tower
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Slide Insurance Holdings, Inc. (SLDE) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “We started off 2026 by delivering another quarter of strong execution across our business,” and growth was “based on strong renewal rates on our existing book, expansion of our voluntary sales and the continued acquisition of Citizens policies” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Bruce Lucas).
  • “We have deliberately built a dynamic coastal specialty insurance platform with the strongest balance sheet in the sector,” and management said it is “accelerat[ing] our geographic expansion throughout 2026” beyond Florida, citing South Carolina momentum and “thoughtful geographic diversification in multiple states” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • On reinsurance, management said it is “in the final process of completing our 2026 reinsurance program,” with completion expected “in the next 1 to 2 weeks,” and added, “we increased our first event reinsurance tower by roughly $1 billion versus 2025,” while “every layer of the reinsurance tower was oversubscribed on favorable terms” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • On capital return, management said, “we completed our $120 million stock repurchase program, and our Board authorized a new $125 million repurchase program,” adding, “the Board of Directors has authorized an additional $100 million share repurchase program” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • “In the first quarter, net income rose 50.8% to $139.5 million… resulting in diluted earnings per share of $1.02,” and “gross premiums written reached $414.8 million, up 49.1% from $278.2 million” (Chief Financial Officer Anastasios Omiridis).

Outlook

  • “We continue to expect gross written premiums between $1.85 billion and $1.95 billion and net income between $455 million and $470 million” (Chief Financial Officer Omiridis).
  • Management framed 2026 growth as “sustained organic expansion, including double-digit increases in policies in force and premium outside of Florida, supplemented by selective opportunities in Florida that meet our targeted returns” (Chief Financial Officer Omiridis).
  • Compared with the prior quarter’s call, management again anchored 2026 growth on “higher policy retentions, higher voluntary sales and the launch of new states,” but Q1 placed more emphasis on voluntary growth being “the larger channel for sure” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).

Financial Results

  • “Gross premiums written reached $414.8 million,” supported by “a 46% year-over-year increase in policies in force, which now stand at 508,928,” and the company said it “acquired an additional $92.3 million in annualized gross premiums or 28,783 policies from Citizens” (Chief Financial Officer Omiridis).
  • “Net losses and loss adjustment expenses totaled $111.1 million,” and management said “our accident year loss ratio improved to 28.4% from 34.2%” (Chief Financial Officer Omiridis).
  • “General and administrative expenses increased to $46.2 million from $41.4 million,” and management reported “an overall expense ratio of 25.1%… and a combined ratio of 55.5%” (Chief Financial Officer Omiridis).
  • Balance sheet and investments were described as “cash and cash equivalents of $1.2 billion and total invested assets of $720 million,” with invested assets “consisting of 33.5% corporate bonds, 31.3% municipal bonds, 24.1% U.S. government bonds and 11.1% asset-backed securities and other” (Chief Financial Officer Omiridis).

Q&A

  • Taylor Scott, Barclays Bank PLC, Research Division: asked whether added reinsurance limit changed the risk profile and how much higher modeled PML would be covered; Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas said, “everything scales in tandem,” and the “tower is at approximately $3.5 billion of first event coverage,” adding that “reinsurance is our single largest expense,” but “the underlying loss ratio should be unchanged.”
  • Jon Paul Newsome, Piper Sandler & Co., Research Division: asked why Slide remains confident in Citizens takeouts and whether competition could rush in; Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas said the Citizens opportunity “is not as robust as it has been,” that Slide is “certainly not reliant on Citizens this year,” and “we think voluntary growth in new states is the real story for ’26,” adding, “we’re not seeing new capital flow in.”
  • Thomas Mcjoynt-Griffith, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods, Inc., Research Division: asked how a major CAT event could affect net income and about multi-event retentions; Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas said retention has “consistently kept… to no more than 25% of pretax earnings,” and described buying “third event cover,” adding, “we do like to step down retentions on event too.”
  • Randy Binner: asked about top-line pacing and seasonality, loss ratio impacts, and elevated cash; Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas said he would “expect top line to steadily increase,” and “you’re going to see an acceleration, particularly in the third and fourth quarters,” adding on losses, “we had some CAT… but there is no PYD,” and on cash, “we are reinvesting those cash proceeds into higher-yielding assets.”
  • Matthew Carletti, Citizens JMP Securities, LLC, Research Division: asked which new states look most attractive and how large California could be; Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas said “#1 for us is definitely California,” the “launch is imminent,” and later added, “we do think there’s an opportunity to grow top line this year by $50 million to $100 million just in California.”
  • Taylor Scott, Barclays Bank PLC, Research Division: asked whether buybacks will continue given valuation and capital; Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas said, “you should expect us to be very active in share buybacks as long as the price is not indicative of fair value.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral-to-skeptical, pressing on catastrophe earnings sensitivity, reinsurance economics, competitive entry, and capital deployment, including questions about “how much higher the modeled PML loss would be,” and what would happen if a hurricane “were to repeat” (Taylor Scott, Barclays; Thomas Mcjoynt-Griffith, KBW).
  • Management’s tone was confident in prepared remarks and firm in Q&A, emphasizing scale and resilience, including “we remain highly confident in our business plan,” and “an event is not some armageddon for us. It’s just a ding to earnings” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • Versus the prior quarter, the dialogue shifted from Q4’s emphasis on favorable development and unusually low storm losses to Q1’s focus on reinsurance tower sizing, event retentions, and imminent multi-state launches, with analysts again probing reinsurance and competition.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 highlighted profitability and underwriting consistency with “combined ratio improving to 55.5%,” while Q4 highlighted an unusually low-quarter outcome with “a quarterly combined ratio of 38%” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • Q1 messaging moved Citizens toward a secondary role as management said “voluntary growth in new states is the real story for ’26,” while Q4 emphasized being “opportunistic with respect to the ongoing Citizens depopulation” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • Q1 provided more concrete reinsurance sizing, stating “approximately $3.5 billion of first event coverage,” while Q4 focused more on the expectation of a “reduction in reinsurance expenses embedded within” guidance ahead of the 6/1 renewal (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).

Risks and Concerns

  • Management flagged market pricing pressure in Florida: “risk-adjusted rate decreases are prevalent in the Florida market and the decreases have been substantial,” while declining to quantify (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • Catastrophe volatility remained central in discussion; management said it targets retention “to no more than 25% of pretax earnings,” and noted it buys “third event cover” with step-down retentions (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).
  • Execution risk in expansion was acknowledged through operational timing language, including California launch being “imminent” and reinsurance completion expected “in the next 1 to 2 weeks” (Founder, CEO & Chairman Lucas).

Final Takeaway

Slide’s management described Q1 as another record quarter and reaffirmed full-year 2026 targets of $1.85 billion to $1.95 billion in gross written premiums and $455 million to $470 million in net income, while positioning voluntary growth in new states—especially an imminent California launch—as the main 2026 growth driver. Management also emphasized an enlarged reinsurance program at about $3.5 billion of first-event coverage and reiterated an aggressive, valuation-driven approach to share repurchases alongside growth investments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Entegris names Sukhi Nagesh as CFO
data.description:
• Entegris (NASDAQ: ENTG) on Thursday said it has appointed Sukhi Nagesh as CFO, effective May 18, 2026.
  • Nagesh joins from Nielsen, where he serves as head of corporate development and M&A.
  • He previously held senior roles at GlobalFoundries and Marvell Technology, among others.
  • He will succeed interim CFO Mike Sauer, who has held the role since March 1 and will continue as vice president and chief accounting officer.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trust3 AI, Dell partner on AI-ready data lakehouse infrastructure
data.description:
• Trust3 AI on Thursday said it has expanded its strategic collaboration with Dell Technologies (DELL) to deliver a secure, governed data lakehouse infrastructure for enterprise AI workloads.
  • The joint solution integrates Trust3 AI’s governance platform into Dell’s data lakehouse architecture, enabling organizations to run analytics and autonomous AI workloads across hybrid and on-premises environments.
  • The offering includes features such as automated sensitive data discovery, centralized policy enforcement, data masking and encryption, and AI auditing aimed at regulatory compliance.
  • The companies said the platform is designed for regulated industries, including financial services, healthcare, and the public sector, with the goal of supporting broader adoption of AI while maintaining data security and compliance.
  • DELL +0.33% premarket to  $206.34.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: T. Rowe Price trades higher as Q1 fees rise despite fall in AUM
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) was trading higher as Q1 investment advisory fees rose even as the Baltimore, Maryland-based asset manager saw a sequential decrease in AUM.

Shares were 0.79% higher at $101.26 during Thursday pre-market trading.

Adjusted diluted EPS of $2.52 rose 13.0% from the year-ago quarter and surpassed the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.35.

Net operating income increased to $680.5M from $596.3M.

Net revenue of $1.86B was up 5.3% year-over-year and above the consensus of $1.85B.

Revenue was boosted by a 5.3% year-over-year growth in investment advisory fees, which reached $1.68B from $1.60B a year ago.

Investment advisory fees represent about 90% of the company's revenue.

Assets under management fell to $1.71B at Q1-end from $1.76B at the end of the fourth quarter of 2025. The company saw net client outflows of $13.7B during the quarter.

Investors from outside the U.S. accounted for 8.6% of the asset manager's AUM as of March 31, 2026, compared to 8.8% at December 31, 2025.

Notably, the investment advisory annualized effective fee rate with performance-based fees fell to 38.6 basis points during the quarter from 39.2 bps in the previous quarter and 40.3 bps a year ago.

"With the recent volatility and broadening of markets, our active management approach positions us to take advantage of the opportunities this climate brings. Our teams are advancing innovative strategies, new vehicles, and compelling solutions to meet the evolving needs of clients," said CEO Rob Sharps.
